profile.name: Lisset Dayana
profile.professionalTitle: BSc in Radiochemistry | Specialist in Computational Chemistry, Molecular Bioactivity and Nuclear Sciences
profile.photoAlt: Professional portrait of Lisset Dayana Gómez Pérez
profile.photoText: Researcher focused on scientific solutions through computational chemistry, biomolecular simulation and applied nuclear technologies.
profile.summary: Researcher oriented toward scientific solutions through molecular modeling, computational chemistry and applied nuclear technologies. Experience in biomolecular simulations, QSAR/QSPR, molecular docking and bioactivity analysis with an interdisciplinary focus across chemistry, physics and computational sciences.

molecular.title: Advanced biomolecular simulation
molecular.description: Visual representation of protein-ligand interactions, catalytic sites, metal coordination, affinity fields and interaction energy.

bioactivity.title: Biological activity maps
bioactivity.description: Visualization of bioactivity, QSAR, pharmacophores, cell migration and molecular networks associated with invasion and angiogenesis.

professionalProfile: I am a Radiochemistry graduate with advanced scientific training in computational chemistry, molecular bioactivity and modeling of nanoscopic systems. My profile combines nuclear sciences, molecular simulation and computational methods applied to the study of bioactive compounds and advanced materials. I stand out for analytical thinking, research capacity and multidisciplinary adaptation, actively participating in scientific projects related to quantum chemistry, molecular dynamics and biomolecular-interaction modeling. I graduated as the top student of my Radiochemistry cohort, with scientific merit recognition for outstanding research results.

researchProjects.title: Modeling of Nanoscopic Structures and Processes for Materials and Bioactivity: Classical, Quantum and Big-Data Methods
researchProjects.description: Participation in research focused on molecular simulation and computational modeling applied to materials and bioactive compounds. Development of structural, energetic and pharmacological analyses through classical, quantum and big-data tools. Institution: Faculty of Chemistry — University of Havana.
results: Application of classical, quantum and big-data methods

details: Strengthening theoretical foundations and computational methods applied to chemical sciences.
Scientific communication for advanced academic training.
Co-founder of a logistics and parcel company.

experience.organization: Eco Hotel, Shopping Center and Recanto da Peixada
details: Development of customer-service, multidisciplinary-work and intercultural-adaptation skills.

education.degree: Padre Félix Varela Institute of Ecclesiastical Studies

certifications: Scientific Merit Award for research results
events: International Congress of Chemistry, Biochemistry and Chemical Engineering — QUIMICUBA 2024
Participation in scientific spaces focused on chemistry, nuclear sciences, computational research and molecular bioactivity.

contact.availability: Available for scientific projects in radiochemistry, computational chemistry, molecular bioactivity, molecular modeling, QSAR/QSPR, molecular dynamics and applied nuclear technologies.
